mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines below 2% signal aggressive monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings. This mechanism operates continuously as a trend signal, with lower rates directly supporting equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.06% as of Nov 2008 reflects recent emergency rate cuts from crisis peak
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.05 over any 12-month rolling window, or SPX produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 2%.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rises above 4.5% signals labor market deterioration that triggers equity repricing downward as investors anticipate lower corporate earnings from reduced consumer spending and demand destruction. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative 20-day returns when unemployment crosses this critical level.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.7% in Nov 2008 but rising sharply from crisis onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 50% of instances within 5 trading days after UNRATE crosses 4.5%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) move together in a demand-side contraction: simultaneous declines in both series signal broad-based loss of consumer and residential demand, reducing near-term corporate revenue expectations and driving negative equity returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: both RSAFS and HOUST decline > 5% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1401k units in Oct 2008, down sharply from pre-crisis levels
failure_condition: Rank IC of interaction term < 0.06 over any 12-month rolling window, or SPX produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines below 102 index points signal supply-side contraction and reduced manufacturing capacity utilization. This persistent weakness predicts negative equity returns over 60 trading days as investors recognize structural demand destruction and earnings downgrades across industrial sectors.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 103.01 in Nov 2008, near crisis lows
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 18-month rolling window, or 60-day forward returns are positive in > 45% of instances when INDPRO < 102.0.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) declines interact with rising unemployment (UNRATE) to signal accelerating labor market deterioration. The joint signal of job losses and rising unemployment rates predicts negative 20-day equity returns as markets price in sustained consumer income destruction and demand weakness.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines > 200k month-over-month AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 156105k in Nov 2008, declining from crisis onset
regime_hint: acute labor market shock with job destruction
failure_condition: Rank IC of interaction term < 0.05 over any 12-month rolling window, or SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 45% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) growth decelerates below 1% annualized rate, signaling economic stagnation that reduces corporate earnings growth expectations. This always-on trend mechanism predicts negative 5-day equity returns as investors continuously reprrice equity risk premiums downward in response to weak growth fundamentals.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 20246.4B (Q3 2008), declining from pre-crisis peak
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 12-month rolling window, or 5-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of instances when GDPC1 growth < 1% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains below 3% while federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is cut aggressively below 2%, creating a regime of real negative rates that incentivizes equity allocation over cash. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts positive 60-day returns when both conditions hold simultaneously.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 303.29 (Nov 2008), FEDFUNDS at 5.06% but declining
failure_condition: Rank IC of interaction term < 0.04 over any 18-month rolling window, or 60-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of instances when both conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) fall below 1200k units annualized, signaling residential demand collapse that predicts negative 20-day equity returns as construction, materials, and financial sectors face earnings headwinds. This threshold-triggered mechanism reflects the housing market's role as a leading indicator of broader economic weakness.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1401k in Oct 2008, approaching crisis lows
regime_hint: housing market collapse with construction sector stress
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 12-month rolling window, or SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances when HOUST < 1200k.
